description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by four wide, dark metal hoops. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The metal hoops are slightly weathered, showing some subtle discoloration and rivet details, especially visible in one view. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with the top appearing to be a fitted lid. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the surface is free of any labels or markings. The overall design suggests it is intended for storage or transport of goods, commonly seen in urban or industrial outdoor settings.
description: A medium-brown wooden barrel with four dark metal hoops, a flat wooden lid, and a rustic, sturdy appearance.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the front, showing the wood grain and three visible metal hoops with no rivets apparent.
description_view_1: This view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the alignment of the metal hoops, with the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_2: The barrel is shown with the metal hoops' rivets visible, adding detail to the reinforcement structure.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the barrel, showing a clean surface with uninterrupted metal hoops and no visible rivets.
